vocab.meaning: học gạo/ôn tập kỹ lưỡng trong thời gian ngắn
vocab.example: Before every history exam, I would bone up on all the significant dates and historical figures to ensure a high score.

vocab.meaning: ôn lại/trau dồi lại kiến thức đã lâu không dùng
vocab.example: I recently decided to brush up on my French, a subject I absolutely adored during my middle school years.
I loved Physics because once the laws of motion finally began to sink in, the whole world started to make sense.

vocab.meaning: kiên trì tiếp tục làm/học điều gì đó khó khăn
vocab.example: Even though Advanced Math was grueling, I kept at it because I found the process of solving complex equations quite rewarding.
Our Literature teacher was brilliant at getting the deeper meaning of classic poems across to the students.

vocab.meaning: học lỏm/học một cách tự nhiên không qua trường lớp
vocab.example: I enjoyed our IT classes, but I actually picked up most of my coding skills just by tinkering with computers at home.

vocab.meaning: đọc nhiều tài liệu để tìm hiểu về một chủ đề
vocab.example: Biology was my favorite subject; I used to spend my weekends reading up on marine ecosystems just for fun.

vocab.meaning: cân nhắc/thử nghiệm (một ý tưởng hoặc chủ đề)
vocab.example: In Art class, I loved to toy with different abstract techniques instead of just following the traditional curriculum.

vocab.meaning: có thể coi là/được chấp nhận như là (một chuyên gia/người giỏi)
vocab.example: I was so obsessed with Geography that I could almost pass for a local guide when discussing European landmarks.

vocab.meaning: nghiên cứu/xem xét kỹ một vấn đề
vocab.example: The reason I favored Chemistry was the opportunity to look into the molecular structure of everyday substances.
